QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_4

question.text: An Investor Service Centre (ISC) operated by an RTA functions as:

answer.text: A single-window system accepting both financial and non-financial transactions
A branch exclusively for processing redemption requests
An online-only portal for digital transactions
A SEBI regional office for investor complaints

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ISCs are physical branches of the RTA that operate as single-window service points. Investors can submit any type of transaction -- purchases, redemptions, switches, address changes, KYC updates -- all at one location. **The Trap:** 'Single-window' doesn't mean only one type of transaction. It means one location handles ALL transaction types, reducing the need for investors to visit multiple offices. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers distribution channels; ISCs are part of the broader service delivery infrastructure that distributors should know about. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Direct walk-in clients to the nearest ISC for any transaction. ISCs of CAMS or KFintech can handle transactions for all AMCs serviced by that RTA -- no need to visit the AMC office.
